methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $129M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
INERATEC, a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Tim Boeltken, has raised $129M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA) operating in climate and energy infrastructure.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, which applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $245M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—KoBold Metals ($3.0B Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B Series C), and Amogy ($700M Series C)—produces a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $700M to $3.0B. These methods imply meaningfully different valuations, reflecting stage and business maturity differences within the peer set.

Triangulating across both methods produces a valuation range of $208M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $623M. The wide spread reflects two material uncertainties: first, INERATEC's position relative to more mature Series C peers whose valuations span nearly 4x; second, the degree to which raised capital should anchor valuation versus pure stage-based multiples. The $623M midpoint sits meaningfully above the stage-progression method but well below the peer median, reflecting INERATEC's earlier stage relative to the comparable set.
